title: Fix GPS capture and camera/photo button in AssetEntryForm

requirements.summary: Fix the camera/photo capture button in AssetEntryForm.tsx so that tapping 'Take Photo' or 'Upload from Gallery' correctly opens the device camera or file picker on mobile devices
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping the camera button on a mobile device opens the device camera to capture a photo.
Tapping the gallery/upload button opens the device file picker to select an image.
Selected or captured photos appear in the photo preview grid within AssetEntryForm.
The form cannot be submitted without at least one photo attached (existing validation preserved).
No JavaScript errors are thrown when interacting with the photo capture/upload controls.

file_operations.description: Fix the file input's accept and capture attributes to ensure proper camera and gallery access on mobile devices. Verify that the file input element has accept='image/*' and capture='environment' for the camera mode. Ensure the hidden file input ref is correctly wired to the onClick handlers of both 'Take Photo' and 'Upload from Gallery' buttons. Add proper event handler wiring to trigger input.click() when buttons are tapped. Test that the onChange event correctly processes selected files and triggers the image compression and preview update flow.

Verify that the PhotoUpload component is correctly integrated and that photo state updates propagate properly. Ensure the existing photo validation logic (requiring at least one photo before submission) remains intact. Confirm that the photos array state is correctly passed to PhotoUpload and that updates from PhotoUpload are reflected in the form's submission payload.

requirements.summary: Fix the GPS capture flow in AssetEntryForm.tsx so that GPS coordinates are reliably obtained on form submission by calling and awaiting the useGeolocation hook's capture function before building the submission payload
acceptanceCriteria: On form submission, the browser prompts the user for location permission if not already granted.
After permission is granted, GPS coordinates (latitude and longitude) are captured and attached to the submission payload.
The submitted asset record stored in the backend contains non-null, accurate GPS latitude and longitude values.
GPS coordinates are displayed on the submission confirmation and in the Admin Dashboard detail view.
If GPS capture fails (e.g., permission denied), a clear error message is shown to the user and submission is blocked or proceeds with a graceful fallback, consistent with existing UX.

file_operations.description: Refactor the form submission handler to call and await the useGeolocation hook's capture() function before constructing the submission payload. Read the resolved GPS coordinates (latitude, longitude, accuracy) directly from the promise result returned by capture(), not from React state. Use the fresh coordinates from the promise to populate the backend payload latitude and longitude fields. Add proper error handling to catch GPS capture failures and display a user-friendly error message using toast. Ensure the submission is blocked if GPS capture fails or provide a graceful fallback consistent with existing UX patterns. Preserve all existing form validation logic including the mandatory photo requirement.

Review the capture function implementation to ensure it returns a properly typed promise containing latitude, longitude, and accuracy. Verify that the Geolocation API call is wrapped correctly and that the promise resolves with fresh coordinate data. Ensure error handling propagates failures (e.g., permission denied, timeout) back to the caller so AssetEntryForm can handle them appropriately.
